response.justification: Szabo exceeds the minimum 15-year experience requirement and demonstrates strong technical leadership across multiple domains. However, the role explicitly requires 'recognized expertise in AI with demonstrated products or platforms built with AI,' which is critically missing from his background. His engagement with AI-agent infrastructure began only 2 months ago (April 2025) and lacks the depth, track record, or supporting credentials (advanced AI/ML degree) necessary for a director-level AI strategy position. While a capable generalist engineer, he is significantly under-qualified for the specialized focus of this role.
strengths: Exceeds minimum requirements with 19 years of engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and infrastructure domains, demonstrating sustained technical growth and adaptability
Proven leadership capability through founding GISCollective, organizing Refactoring Dojos, creating educational workshops, and mentoring through open-source projects
Strong technical breadth with demonstrated ability to learn and implement diverse technologies (TypeScript, DLang, cloud infrastructure) across multiple problem domains
concerns: No demonstrated AI expertise or track record building AI products despite the role requiring 'recognized expertise in AI'—recent engagement with AI-agent infrastructure (April 2025) is too new to establish credibility
Lacks advanced degree in AI/ML field (preferred requirement); career background is in traditional software engineering with no published work or thought leadership in AI/ML domains

No evidence of director-level strategic influence, organizational impact at scale, or recognized industry expertise in AI or cloud strategy—primarily demonstrated as IC contributor rather than strategic leader

key_factors.factor: 19 years of engineering experience exceeding minimum requirement
Recent unproven transition to AI infrastructure work
